name: Mount Abu
description: Mount Abu is a magnificent destination in Rajasthan, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Mount Abu offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Mount Abu boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Dilwara Jain Temples
topAttractions.description: Widely regarded as the finest example of marble carving in the world — a complex of 5 Jain temples (11th-13th century CE). The Vimala Vasahi (1031 CE) and Luna Vasahi (1230 CE) are the finest, with incredible intricacy in the pillars, domes, and sculptures — the ceiling medallions took 14 years each to carve. No photography permitted inside. Entry: Free (donations welcome). Open 12PM-6PM daily.

topAttractions.name: Nakki Lake
topAttractions.description: A sacred lake (1200m altitude) at the center of Mount Abu town, legend says excavated by the gods with their nails (nakh in Hindi, hence Nakki). Boating available (₹150/30 min). The Toad Rock formation overlooking the lake is a climbing spot.

topAttractions.name: Guru Shikhar
topAttractions.description: At 1722m, the highest peak in the Aravalli Range and the highest point in Rajasthan. A 15km drive from town followed by 300 steps. The Guru Dattatreya temple at the top is ancient. Views extend to Gujarat on clear days.

topAttractions.name: Achalgarh Fort
topAttractions.description: A 14th-century fort (1452 CE) built by Rana Kumbha, with the Achaleshwar Mahadev Temple (ancient Shiva temple with footprint of Lord Shiva and a natural Shiva lingam). Mandakini and Agni Kund lakes are nearby.

topAttractions.name: Sunset Point
topAttractions.description: The most popular viewpoint in Mount Abu — a terraced hillside looking west towards the Gujarat plains. Becomes extremely crowded at sunset (October-March). Horse rides to the point (₹200-400). Despite the crowds, the sunsets over the Aravalli plains are genuinely magnificent.

topAttractions.name: Wildlife Sanctuary (Trevor's Tank)
topAttractions.description: A 288 sq km wildlife sanctuary surrounding Mount Abu — home to leopards, sambar, crocodiles (at Trevor's Tank — a crocodile viewing point 5km from town). The sanctuary has excellent birdwatching: paradise flycatcher, grey jungle fowl, painted spurfowl.

culture: The culture of Mount Abu is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
